howItWorks.heading: How It Works
howItWorks.subheading: From corridor check to customs clearance in four steps.

step1.label: KNOW
step1.title: Map your shipment corridor
step1.description: TapTrao identifies the documents, duties, and regulatory triggers for your route — including EUDR, CBAM, SPS, CITES, and more.

step2.label: ASK
step2.title: Collect documents from suppliers
step2.description: Send a zero-login upload link. Your supplier sees exactly what to provide and uploads directly from their phone.

step3.label: PROVE
step3.title: Cross-check everything before it moves
step3.description: TapTrao checks the full picture — certificates of origin, phytosanitary certificates, LC terms, customs declarations, and supporting paperwork — flagging every gap before the shipment leaves port.

step4.label: MONITOR
step4.title: Stay ahead of changes
step4.description: Get alerts when regulations change, deadlines approach, or new obligations affect your corridor.

twinlog.heading: TwinLog: proof you complied
twinlog.subheading: Every check, every document, every supplier submission — permanently recorded and tamper-proof.
twinlog.body: If a customs authority, bank, buyer, or auditor ever questions your shipment, you have verifiable proof of what was checked, when it was checked, and who provided it.
twinlog.eudr: Your EUDR TwinLog — hashed and exportable as PDF.
twinlog.cbam: Your CBAM TwinLog — hashed and exportable as PDF.
twinlog.record: Your complete compliance record — yours to keep and present on demand.
twinlog.closing: TapTrao doesn't just help you comply. It helps you prove you complied.

supplierPreview.subheading: A simple upload link. No login. No confusion.
supplierPreview.cta: Run a free preview to see it in action

factsSection.heading: What every importer should know
factsSection.subheading: Upcoming deadlines, real compliance cost examples, and which regulations apply to your corridors.

regulations.heading: Key regulations
regulations.eudr: EUDR — Entry into application now for large operators. Cocoa, coffee, palm oil, rubber, soy, wood, cattle. Missing a Due Diligence Statement means your shipment doesn't clear.
regulations.cbam: CBAM — Fully operational from January 2026. Importers must purchase certificates reflecting embedded carbon. Affects fertilizers, cement, aluminium, iron and steel.
regulations.ukBtom: UK BTOM — Full SPS checks on all food and plant imports. A missing phytosanitary certificate means automatic rejection at the border.

costs.heading: Cost of compliance failures
costs.demurrage: $75–$300/day demurrage at Felixstowe, Rotterdam, Hamburg while documents are stuck
costs.bankFee: $95–$160 bank fee for a single LC discrepancy
costs.isfPenalty: $5,000 US ISF late-filing penalty per violation
costs.cascadeCost: A single missing Certificate of Origin from Ghana, Côte d'Ivoire, or Kenya can cascade into $1,500+ in delays and fees
